zh-tw.title: 失眠語錄
zh-tw.description: 凌晨三點的腦內小劇場，比白天還熱鬧。給所有正在床上跟天花板對眼的人。

en.title: Insomnia Quotes
en.description: The 3am brain theater is busier than rush hour. For everyone currently making eye contact with their ceiling.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by classic 'I have internet not insomnia' format; rewritten to focus on phone battery as the modern hook

zh-tw.content:
別人睡不著是因為失眠。
我睡不著是因為，手機還有電。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨兩點還在滑短影音迴圈時的最佳自嘲。發限動或群組，會收到一片「同款」回應。
variations: 我沒有失眠，只是手機沒沒電而已。

en.content:
Other people can't sleep because they have insomnia.
I can't sleep because my phone still has battery.
en.editorNote: Use this at 2am when you're three reels deep into the doomscroll. Post it to your story and watch the 'same' replies roll in.
variations: I don't have insomnia. My phone just isn't dead yet.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I stay up so tomorrow me will hate today me' format; rewritten with new framing

zh-tw.content:
我熬夜，是為了讓明天的我，恨今天的我。
這就是我的自我照護。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合貼在已經滑到三點還沒睡的時候。「自我照護」這個詞用反話，特別有共鳴。
variations: 今天的我繼續熬，明天的我繼續罵，我們是一個完整的循環。

en.content:
I stay up late so tomorrow me will hate today me.
That's my version of self-care.
en.editorNote: Best used when you're already past 3am and still scrolling. The ironic 'self-care' framing is what makes it land.
variations: Today me stays up. Tomorrow me suffers. We're a complete ecosystem.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'brain replays cringe memories at night' meme format; rewritten with specific 8th grade angle

zh-tw.content:
晚上12點：好，今天就到這，要睡了。
凌晨1點：欸對了，國二那次我講錯話，不知道對方還記不記得？
zh-tw.editorNote: 腦袋在睡前突然開放「黑歷史回顧頻道」的時候。傳給朋友會引發一連串自己的回憶湧現。
variations: 腦袋說：今天先講到這。然後從第一件丟臉的事開始重播。

en.content:
12am: Okay, that's enough for today, time to sleep.
1am: Oh by the way, that dumb thing I said in 8th grade — wonder if they still remember?
en.editorNote: For when your brain opens the 'cringe replay' channel right at bedtime. Send to a friend and watch them spiral into their own memories.
variations: Brain: Okay, calling it a day. Brain, ten seconds later: But about that thing in 2014...

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'at 3am I have two moods' format; rewritten with absurd zoological example

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點的我，有兩個選擇：
寫出一本暢銷書，或上網查松鼠到底會不會做夢。
通常我選後者。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨三點的腦袋處於「天才與廢人」的疊加狀態。用來描述自己又一次選了 Google 怪問題的時刻。
variations: 凌晨三點的選擇：改變人生，或查為什麼袋鼠不會打嗝。

en.content:
Me at 3am has two options:
Write a bestseller, or google whether squirrels actually dream.
Usually it's the second one.
en.editorNote: The 3am brain exists in a superposition of genius and disaster. Use this when you've once again chosen weird Google searches over greatness.
variations: 3am choices: change your life, or look up why kangaroos can't burp.

items.sourceNote: Original take on the classic 'counting sheep' joke; rewritten with rebellion angle

zh-tw.content:
失眠時我認真數羊。
數到第57隻才發現，第3隻已經跳回來，正瞪著我看。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「數羊真的有用嗎」這件事的最佳吐槽。適合一邊數羊一邊翻身的人發朋友圈。
variations: 我數的羊已經組成工會，正抗議我加班。

en.content:
I take counting sheep seriously.
By sheep #57, I notice sheep #3 has already jumped back over the fence and is glaring at me.
en.editorNote: The definitive takedown of 'just count sheep.' Perfect for the person who's tossing and turning while still trying.
variations: My sheep have formed a union and are protesting my unpaid overtime.

items.sourceNote: Original countdown structure inspired by sleep math meme; rewritten with capitulation ending

zh-tw.content:
12點：再8小時要起床。
1點：再7小時。
2點：再6小時。
3點：算了，反正起不來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠時不停看時鐘倒數的全過程。上班族特別有感，傳同事群組會引發共鳴。
variations: 倒數的不是時間，是隔天的痛苦。

en.content:
12am: 8 hours until the alarm.
1am: 7 hours.
2am: 6 hours.
3am: Forget it, I won't be getting up anyway.
en.editorNote: The full clock-watching countdown. Hits especially hard for office workers — drop it in the team chat for instant solidarity.
variations: It's not the hours counting down. It's tomorrow's suffering.

items.sourceNote: Original aphorism on the 'bed is most comfortable when alarm rings' phenomenon

zh-tw.content: 床最舒服的那一秒，永遠是鬧鐘響的那一秒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 短而有力的金句，適合上班族貼便利貼或設成桌布。傳給賴床戰友，他們會懂。
variations: 棉被的吸力，跟你需要起床的時間成正比。

en.content: The bed is at its most comfortable in the exact second the alarm goes off.
en.editorNote: Short, sharp, and universally true. Slap it on a sticky note or send it to your fellow snooze-button warriors.
variations: The blanket's gravity is proportional to how much you need to get up.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle/healing-tone item; balances the humor-heavy collection with permission-giving comfort

zh-tw.content:
如果今晚真的睡不著，沒關係。
你不用解釋，也不用解決。
躺著，也算是休息。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合傳給最近睡不好、又一直被叫去看醫生的朋友。這句話不催他，只是告訴他「躺著就好」。
variations: 睡不著也是一種狀態，不是錯誤。

en.content:
If you really can't sleep tonight, that's okay.
You don't have to explain it, or fix it.
Just lying there counts as rest.
en.editorNote: For a friend who's been sleeping poorly and keeps getting told to 'see a doctor.' This doesn't push them — it just gives them permission to lie down.
variations: Not sleeping is a state, not a mistake.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'doomscrolling till sunrise' format; rewritten with absurd useless-skill twist

zh-tw.content:
我以為再滑五分鐘就會想睡。
結果天亮了，我學會三種我這輩子用不到的縫紉技巧。
zh-tw.editorNote: 演算法把你帶到奇怪領域時的最佳自我介紹。引發共鳴：誰沒有半夜學過根本不會用的東西？
variations: 再五分鐘就睡 × 6 小時 = 我會做窯燒了。

en.content:
I thought five more minutes of scrolling would make me sleepy.
The sun came up and I now know three sewing techniques I'll never use.
en.editorNote: The perfect caption for when the algorithm takes you somewhere strange. Everyone has learned something useless at 4am.
variations: 'Just five more minutes' × 6 hours = I now know how to fire pottery.

items.sourceNote: Original take on the coffee-insomnia loop; rewritten as 'perpetual motion machine' punchline

zh-tw.content:
白天喝咖啡，晚上睡不著。
晚上睡不著，白天更需要咖啡。
我終於成為一台 永動機。
zh-tw.editorNote: 咖啡因循環的完美說明。適合傳到辦公室群組，咖啡黨同事一定按愛心。
variations: 我不是被咖啡綁架，我跟它是合夥關係。

en.content:
Coffee in the morning so I won't sleep at night.
No sleep means I need more coffee tomorrow.
I have officially become a perpetual motion machine.
en.editorNote: The complete caffeine loop, summarized. Drop it in the office chat — your coffee-addicted coworkers will react instantly.
variations: I'm not held hostage by coffee. We're business partners.

items.sourceNote: Original 廢話文學 format applied to insomnia — stating the obvious as if profound

zh-tw.content: 睡不著的人，最大的特徵，就是還醒著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 廢話文學經典結構，講了等於沒講。適合在群組講完真實的失眠原因之後當收尾。
variations: 失眠的本質，是你應該睡的時候沒在睡。

en.content: The defining feature of someone who can't sleep is that they're still awake.
en.editorNote: Classic absurdist 'nonsense literature' format — says everything by saying nothing. Great as a closer after a real insomnia rant.
variations: The essence of insomnia is not sleeping when you should be sleeping.

items.sourceNote: Original observation on 3am online shopping; rewritten with absurd specific item

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點下的訂單，七天後到貨。
那時的我，已經完全想不起來，自己為什麼需要一隻會發光的橡皮鴨。
zh-tw.editorNote: 深夜衝動購物的標準流程描述。適合貼在開箱了奇怪商品的限動上。
variations: 深夜購物車裡的東西，是另一個我選的。我現在不認識他。

en.content:
Ordered at 3am, arrived in seven days.
By then I had absolutely no memory of why I needed a glowing rubber duck.
en.editorNote: The full life cycle of a late-night impulse buy. Caption material for the unboxing of something baffling.
variations: The things in my late-night cart were chosen by a different person. I no longer know him.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 發瘋文學 dramatic insomnia framing; rewritten as corporate meeting metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我不是失眠，我是在跟我的大腦開長會。
議程：那件十年前的小事。
結論：明天再開一次。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把腦內反芻包裝成「會議」的職場式自嘲。上班族失眠時用，特別貼切。
variations: 我跟大腦的會議從來沒有結論，只有續會。

en.content:
I'm not insomniac, I'm in a long meeting with my brain.
Agenda: that small thing from ten years ago.
Resolution: reconvene tomorrow.
en.editorNote: Reframes nighttime overthinking as a workplace meeting. Especially relatable for office workers lying awake.
variations: My meetings with my brain never reach a conclusion. They just get rescheduled.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — pairs with iq-008 for the healing register

zh-tw.content:
翻來覆去的時候，先別急著生自己的氣。
身體只是還沒準備好降落，多繞兩圈也可以。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠時最容易陷入「為什麼睡不著」的二次焦慮。這句話讓人允許自己慢一點。傳給對自己很嚴格的朋友。
variations: 睡不著不是身體故障，只是它今天想晚點下班。

en.content:
When you're tossing and turning, don't rush to be mad at yourself.
Your body just isn't ready to land. A few extra laps are allowed.
en.editorNote: Insomnia loves to spiral into 'why can't I sleep' anxiety. This gives permission to slow down. Send to a friend who's hard on themselves.
variations: Not sleeping isn't a malfunction. Your body just wants to clock out late tonight.

items.sourceNote: Original 'just five more minutes' aphorism with political-style framing

zh-tw.content:
「再五分鐘就睡」，是世界上最不負責任的承諾。
簽署人從來沒履行過，而且年年連任。
zh-tw.editorNote: 用政治承諾比喻熬夜的內心戲。短句結構俏皮，適合限動或當每日金句。
variations: 「再五分鐘」是熬夜界的萬用通行證。

en.content:
"Just five more minutes" is the world's most irresponsible promise.
The signer never delivers, and somehow gets re-elected every year.
en.editorNote: Treats the late-night negotiation with yourself like a broken political pledge. Snappy structure, perfect for stories or a daily-quote slot.
variations: 'Five more minutes' is the universal passport in Insomnia Land.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '3am brain content breakdown' meme; rewritten with localized specific percentages and items

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點，我的腦袋裡：
40% 是國小校歌副歌，
30% 是高中數學一題我到現在還不會的題目，
剩下的是想吃鹹酥雞但已經沒開了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 深夜腦內混亂大雜燴的最佳分類。傳給也常常失眠的朋友，他們會自動補上自己的清單。
variations: 三點的腦袋目錄：童年廣告歌、丟臉回憶、想吃宵夜。

en.content:
At 3am, the contents of my brain:
40% the chorus of my elementary school song,
30% one math problem from high school I still can't solve,
the rest is wanting fried chicken from a place that closed two hours ago.
en.editorNote: The perfect breakdown of late-night brain chaos. Send to a fellow insomniac and they'll instantly start listing their own.
variations: 3am brain index: jingles from childhood, cringe memories, craving snacks that are unavailable.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '與其被動失眠，不如主動熬夜' format; rewritten with reframing of agency

zh-tw.content:
與其說我失眠，不如說我提前領取明天的疲憊。
這叫超前部署。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把熬夜講得很有規劃感的反諷金句。適合貼在已經知道明天會很累但還是不睡的時候。
variations: 我不是睡不著，我是在為明天的黑眼圈打底。

en.content:
I'm not having insomnia. I'm just collecting tomorrow's exhaustion in advance.
Call it strategic planning.
en.editorNote: Reframes staying up as a deliberate, well-planned operation. Perfect caption for when you know you'll regret it but you're still scrolling.
variations: I'm not awake. I'm pre-loading the dark circles for tomorrow.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'brain reviews worst decisions at 3am' format; rewritten with annual-review corporate metaphor

zh-tw.content:
我的大腦每天凌晨都會主動加班，
項目是：今年所有出包時刻年度回顧。
而且不發加班費。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把深夜反芻包裝成「無償加班」，職場人特別有感。傳到同事群組會引起一片苦笑。
variations: 我的腦袋是公司裡最認真的員工，問題是它的業務是審我。

en.content:
My brain works overtime every night,
project: annual review of every embarrassing moment this year.
It doesn't even pay me overtime.
en.editorNote: Late-night rumination dressed up as unpaid corporate overtime. Drop it in the work chat — your coworkers will laugh through gritted teeth.
variations: My brain is the most dedicated employee in the company. Unfortunately its job is auditing me.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'brain offers selection of worst memories' format; rewritten as in-flight service announcement

zh-tw.content:
大腦：歡迎搭乘失眠號航班。
本班機提供五年前的尷尬時刻、未回的訊息、以及一個你以為早就忘了的人。
敬請享用。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把深夜腦內噪音包裝成航空公司廣播，特別有畫面感。適合在凌晨翻來覆去時當限動文案。
variations: 本日特餐：高中那次當眾跌倒、配上沒回的告白訊息。

en.content:
Brain: Welcome aboard Flight Insomnia.
Tonight's menu includes one cringe moment from five years ago, an unanswered text, and someone you thought you'd forgotten.
Enjoy your flight.
en.editorNote: Frames the late-night noise as an in-flight announcement. Surprisingly cinematic — perfect caption for the toss-and-turn hour.
variations: Tonight's special: that public faceplant in 2019, paired with a confession text you never sent.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my brain has a to-do list from 2007' format; rewritten with project-management framing

zh-tw.content:
我的腦袋有一份 2014 年就該結案的待辦清單。
它每晚都拿出來開檢討會。
沒有結論，但很準時。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「為什麼我都在想十年前的小事」的最佳吐槽。失眠加完美主義者必收。
variations: 我的大腦把過去當未完成專案，每晚開站立會議。

en.content:
My brain has a to-do list that should have closed in 2014.
It pulls it out for review every single night.
No conclusions. But always on time.
en.editorNote: The definitive comeback to 'why am I still thinking about something from a decade ago.' For the insomniac perfectionists.
variations: My brain treats the past as an open project and holds a nightly standup.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'sleep is a luxury' framing; rewritten with subscription-service metaphor

zh-tw.content:
睡眠這個訂閱方案，我每個月都有付費，
但系統一直顯示「連線失敗」。
客服在哪？
zh-tw.editorNote: 把失眠包裝成訂閱服務故障的科技梗，年輕族群秒懂。適合在床上滑手機的時候發。
variations: 我有付月費，怎麼還是看不到睡眠這個內容？

en.content:
I pay the monthly fee for the sleep subscription,
but the system keeps showing "connection failed."
Where's customer support?
en.editorNote: Insomnia repackaged as a streaming service outage. Lands instantly with anyone who lives online. Caption for that mid-toss scroll.
variations: I'm paying full price for sleep and the content still won't load.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '3am brain asks weird questions' meme; rewritten with localized random question set

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點的腦袋會問：
章魚睡覺的時候會變色嗎？
如果地球停止自轉，我會不會比較好睡？
我國小三年級的同桌現在過得好嗎？
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「腦袋為什麼會在最不該想事情的時候開派對」的精準描繪。傳給也常常胡思亂想的朋友。
variations: 三點的腦袋有自己的 Google：問的全是查不到的。

en.content:
Questions my brain asks at 3am:
Do octopuses change color in their sleep?
Would I sleep better if Earth stopped spinning?
Is my third-grade desk partner doing okay these days?
en.editorNote: A precise portrait of why your brain throws a party at the worst possible hour. Send it to the friend whose mind also won't shut off.
variations: 3am brain has its own Google. The queries return nothing.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — extends the healing register established by iq-008 and iq-014

zh-tw.content:
睡不著的夜，不一定要有意義。
你可以只是醒著，然後等天亮。
那也是一種完成。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠時最大的壓力，是覺得「我必須做點什麼」。這句話拆掉那個壓力。傳給對「夜晚」有罪惡感的朋友。
variations: 醒著到天亮，也是一種把夜晚走完的方式。

en.content:
A sleepless night doesn't have to mean anything.
You can just be awake, and wait for morning.
That counts as finishing the night too.
en.editorNote: The hardest part of insomnia is feeling you should be doing something with it. This dismantles that pressure. For the friend who feels guilty about being awake.
variations: Staying awake until sunrise is also a way to see the night through.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'awake when the world sleeps = secret agent' framing; rewritten with localized mission detail

zh-tw.content:
全世界都在睡的時候，只有我醒著。
感覺自己像個秘密特工，
任務內容是：思考冰箱裡那塊蛋糕還能不能吃。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把獨自清醒的孤寂感包裝成特務電影。深夜偷吃宵夜的人會狂笑，發限動很有畫面感。
variations: 我是夜班的代理人，工作項目：什麼都沒做。

en.content:
When the whole world is asleep, only I'm awake.
I feel like a secret agent on a mission,
and the mission is: figure out if that cake in the fridge is still edible.
en.editorNote: Reframes the lonely 3am awake feeling as a spy thriller. Late-night snackers will lose it. Cinematic caption material for a story post.
variations: I'm the agent on the night shift. Mission objective: absolutely nothing.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'scrolling memes at 2am isn't insomnia, it's research' format; rewritten with academic-paper framing

zh-tw.content:
我不是在滑手機，我是在做田野調查。
研究主題：為什麼演算法總在凌晨兩點，推薦我看貓咪洗澡。
論文進度：失敗。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把熬夜滑手機講得很有學術感的反諷。研究生、上班族都會有感，傳到群組會被截圖。
variations: 這不是熬夜，這是長期觀察人類行為的實驗，受試者是我自己。

en.content:
I'm not scrolling, I'm conducting field research.
Research topic: why does the algorithm always recommend cats taking baths at 2am?
Dissertation progress: failure.
en.editorNote: Repackages doom-scrolling as serious academic work. Grad students and office workers will both nod. Highly screenshot-able.
variations: It's not insomnia, it's a longitudinal study of human behavior. The subject is me.

items.sourceNote: Original Sunday-night-dread angle; not covered in existing items

zh-tw.content:
週日晚上11點：今天好好放鬆。
週日晚上11點半：明天的會議要講什麼？
週日晚上12點：我為什麼還在這家公司。
週日凌晨2點：我為什麼還活著。
zh-tw.editorNote: 週日夜晚的「逐級下沉」式焦慮路徑。社畜共鳴值爆表，傳同事群組會引發一片苦笑跟貼圖。
variations: 週日晚上的失眠，是對週一的提前致敬。

en.content:
Sunday 11pm: Tonight I'm really going to relax.
Sunday 11:30pm: What am I going to say in tomorrow's meeting?
Sunday midnight: Why am I still at this company?
Sunday 2am: Why am I still alive?
en.editorNote: The full Sunday-night descent in four lines. Lethal accuracy for office workers — drop in the team chat for a chorus of grim emojis.
variations: Sunday-night insomnia is just an advance tribute to Monday.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'time off the clock' metaphor to the healing register

zh-tw.content:
睡不著的夜晚，就當作偷來的一段時間。
沒有人會檢查你怎麼用，
你可以發呆，也可以什麼都不做。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把失眠重新框架成「多出來的時間」，移除「應該睡著」的壓力。傳給最近因為睡不著而焦慮的朋友，會讓他鬆一口氣。
variations: 醒著的這幾個小時，是世界送你的，不用拿來焦慮。

en.content:
Treat a sleepless night as stolen time.
Nobody's going to check how you use it.
You can stare at the wall. You can do nothing at all.
en.editorNote: Reframes insomnia as bonus hours instead of failure. Lifts the 'I should be asleep' pressure. Send to a friend who's been anxious about not sleeping — they'll exhale.
variations: These extra hours are a gift from the world. You don't have to spend them on worry.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my relationship with sleep is toxic but committed' format; rewritten with breakup-text framing

zh-tw.content:
我跟睡眠的關係，是那種：
每晚都說好今天要早點見面，
結果它放我鴿子，我還是癡癡等到天亮。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把失眠講成單戀的渣男關係，特別有畫面感。適合在又一次被睡眠放鳥的清晨發限動。
variations: 睡眠對我若即若離，我們就快十年了還在曖昧期。

en.content:
My relationship with sleep is the kind where:
every night we agree to meet up early,
then it ghosts me and I still wait by the door until sunrise.
en.editorNote: Frames insomnia as a one-sided situationship. Perfect caption for the morning after sleep stood you up again.
variations: Sleep keeps me on read. We've been in this almost-relationship for a decade.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'brain starts a podcast with zero replay value' tweet format; rewritten with localized podcast details

zh-tw.content:
我一閉上眼，腦袋就自動開播 Podcast。
節目名稱：你今天那句話講錯了嗎？
第 4,728 集，零訂閱，但每天準時更新。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「腦袋睡前一定要碎念」的精準描繪。傳給也常常被腦內節目纏住的朋友，會笑著傳「我也訂閱了」。
variations: 我的腦袋開的節目沒有廣告，但有無止盡的重播。

en.content:
The moment I close my eyes, my brain auto-launches a podcast.
Show title: Did you say something weird today?
Episode 4,728. Zero subscribers. Drops on schedule every night.
en.editorNote: Pinpoint description of the pre-sleep mental noise. Send to a friend whose brain also runs its own broadcast — they'll reply 'subscribed.'
variations: My brain's podcast has no ads. Just endless reruns.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'thoughts at night are like pop-up ads I can't close' format; rewritten with software bug framing

zh-tw.content:
深夜的念頭，就像關不掉的彈出視窗。
你按了三次 X，它跳出五個新的。
而且每個都連到你最不想看的回憶。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡前思緒包裝成網頁彈窗的科技梗，數位原住民秒懂。適合在被腦內訊息轟炸到睡不著的時候發。
variations: 我的腦袋沒有廣告攔截，深夜流量爆表。

en.content:
Thoughts at night are like pop-up ads you can't close.
You click the X three times, five more appear,
and every one of them links to the memory you wanted to avoid.
en.editorNote: Repackages racing thoughts as a malware-infested browser tab. Hits instantly with anyone online. Drop it when your brain refuses to quit.
variations: My brain has no ad blocker. The night-shift traffic is brutal.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — pairs the healing register with a specific 'permission to be tired tomorrow' angle

zh-tw.content:
如果今晚真的睡不好，明天就放自己一馬。
少做一點事，多喝一點水，
累的人，不需要再考核自己。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人最怕「明天還是要正常運轉」的壓力。這句話直接給出許可。傳給最近睡眠很糟又硬撐上班的朋友。
variations: 睡不好的隔天，不用補回來。可以就讓自己慢一天。

en.content:
If tonight really doesn't go well, cut yourself some slack tomorrow.
Do a little less, drink a little more water.
Tired people don't need to grade themselves.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs dread the 'I still have to function tomorrow' pressure. This gives explicit permission to ease up. For the friend who's been pushing through bad sleep.
variations: The day after bad sleep, you don't have to make it up. You're allowed to go slow.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'permanently exhausted pigeon' classic line; rewritten with a localized self-classification framing

zh-tw.content:
我不是早起的鳥，也不是熬夜的貓頭鷹。
我是一隻長期睡不飽的鴿子，
白天眼神渙散，半夜眼睛超亮。
zh-tw.editorNote: 經典「永久疲倦鴿子」梗的在地化版本。適合在自介、限動描述自己睡眠週期完全壞掉的狀態。
variations: 我不屬於早起派也不屬於夜貓派，我屬於累爆派。

en.content:
I'm not an early bird, and I'm not a night owl either.
I'm a permanently sleep-deprived pigeon —
glassy-eyed by day, weirdly alert at 3am.
en.editorNote: A local riff on the famous 'permanently exhausted pigeon' line. Perfect bio material or story caption when your sleep cycle is fully broken.
variations: I'm not a morning person or a night person. I'm an 'I'm wrecked' person.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'accidentally awake at 4am' meme format; rewritten with a 'mistakenly clocked into another shift' angle

zh-tw.content:
凌晨四點不小心醒來，
感覺自己被排到一個不該上的班，
更慘的是，這份工作沒有薪水。
zh-tw.editorNote: 「凌晨四點突然清醒」的精準描述。社畜共鳴度爆表，傳同事群組會有一片「我也常常被排這班」的回應。
variations: 四點的清醒，是身體擅自幫我加的一個班。

en.content:
Accidentally awake at 4am.
Feels like I've been scheduled for a shift I never agreed to —
and worse, it doesn't pay anything.
en.editorNote: Pinpoint description of the random 4am wake-up. Lethal for office workers — drop it in the team chat for an instant chorus of 'same shift, every night.'
variations: 4am awake-mode is a shift my body assigned me without asking.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'time travel by staring at the ceiling' line; rewritten with a 'wrong-direction time machine' twist

zh-tw.content:
我終於發明了時光機。
操作方法是：躺在床上盯著天花板。
效果是：時間倒退回我十年前的丟臉時刻。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把失眠講成「壞掉的時光機」的反諷金句。適合在又被腦內回憶拉回過去的清晨發限動，配張黑色的天花板照片很搭。
variations: 我的時光機只往一個方向走，而且永遠停在我最不想去的那一站。

en.content:
I've finally invented a time machine.
Instructions: lie in bed and stare at the ceiling.
Result: time rewinds straight to my most embarrassing moment from ten years ago.
en.editorNote: Reframes insomnia as a malfunctioning time machine. Perfect early-morning caption, especially paired with a moody ceiling photo.
variations: My time machine only goes one direction, and it always stops at the stop I least want to visit.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'company in the dark' angle to the healing register

zh-tw.content:
如果你現在還醒著，
那我們算是一起在這個夜晚裡。
不一定要做什麼，知道有人陪著，就夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的孤獨感，比睡不著本身更難受。這句話只是輕輕說「我也在」。傳給最近常常一個人撐到天亮的朋友。
variations: 醒著的人不一定孤單，至少現在我也在。

en.content:
If you're still awake right now,
then we're sharing this night together.
You don't have to do anything. Just knowing someone else is here is enough.
en.editorNote: The loneliness of insomnia is often worse than the lack of sleep. This just quietly says 'me too.' Send to a friend who's been making it to sunrise alone.
variations: Being awake doesn't have to mean alone. At least right now, I'm here too.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the classic 'strong relationship with my ceiling' line; rewritten with a localized 'long-term contract' framing

zh-tw.content:
我跟我房間的天花板，已經是穩定交往的關係。
每晚見面三小時，從來不冷場。
它沒講過話，但我覺得它都懂。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把每晚瞪天花板講成感情長跑，特別有畫面。適合在又一次熬到看天花板的清晨發限動，配張天花板照很搭。
variations: 天花板是我目前最穩定的感情對象，已經五年了。

en.content:
Me and my ceiling are in a long-term committed relationship.
We meet up for three hours every night and it's never awkward.
It's never said a word, but I'm pretty sure it gets me.
en.editorNote: Frames the nightly ceiling-stare as a stable long-term relationship. Cinematic. Pair it with a photo of your actual ceiling for the perfect 4am post.
variations: My ceiling is the most stable relationship I have. Five years strong.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'brain needs a mute button' format; rewritten with a localized customer-service complaint framing

zh-tw.content:
我想跟製造商客訴一下：
我的大腦沒有靜音鍵，沒有暫停鍵，
連音量旋鈕都壞了，只會自己越轉越大聲。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把腦袋無法停機的痛苦包裝成 3C 產品瑕疵申訴。理工同事跟資深失眠戶都會懂，傳群組會有人接「我這台還會自動更新」。
variations: 我的腦袋是出廠瑕疵品，過保了也找不到人修。

en.content:
I'd like to file a complaint with the manufacturer:
my brain has no mute button, no pause button,
and the volume knob is broken — it only turns itself up.
en.editorNote: Treats the unstoppable late-night brain as a defective electronics product. Hits engineers and chronic insomniacs alike. Expect a 'mine auto-updates' reply.
variations: My brain shipped with a manufacturing defect, and the warranty expired years ago.

items.sourceNote: Original take on the smart-watch sleep score phenomenon; not covered by existing items

zh-tw.content:
手錶今早跟我說：你昨晚的睡眠分數是 32 分。
謝謝你，我自己也很清楚。
還需要你提醒嗎？
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「智慧穿戴在你最虛弱的時候補一刀」的精準吐槽。戴 Apple Watch、Garmin 的同事一定共鳴。
variations: 我花錢買手錶，不是為了讓它每天早上 8 點公開羞辱我。

en.content:
My watch this morning: your sleep score last night was 32.
Thanks, I'm well aware.
Did I ask?
en.editorNote: A precise jab at the smart-watch finishing-move it pulls when you're already wrecked. Anyone with an Apple Watch or Garmin will nod hard.
variations: I didn't pay for this watch so it could roast me at 8am every day.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'don't look at the clock' anxiety spiral tweets; rewritten with a mental-math punchline

zh-tw.content:
規則一：不要看時鐘。
規則二：千萬不要算還剩幾小時可以睡。
（三秒後）那如果現在馬上睡著，是不是還有四小時又二十七分？
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠時最經典的「越告訴自己別算越會算」迴圈。傳給也常常在床上做心算的朋友，他們會回「我剛剛也在算」。
variations: 我答應自己不看時鐘，結果默默開始用窗外天色估算時間。

en.content:
Rule one: don't look at the clock.
Rule two: do NOT calculate how many hours of sleep are left.
(three seconds later) Okay but if I fall asleep right now, that's still four hours and twenty-seven minutes, right?
en.editorNote: The classic 'the more you tell yourself not to count, the more you count' loop. Send to the friend who also does bedtime math — they'll reply 'just did this.'
variations: I promised myself not to check the clock. Now I'm estimating the time from how light the window looks.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'the night owes you nothing, and you owe it nothing' angle to the healing register

zh-tw.content:
夜晚沒有欠你一個好覺，
你也沒有欠夜晚一個努力入睡的樣子。
關燈、躺好，就已經夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人最容易陷入「我必須努力睡著」的迴圈，反而更睡不著。這句話把「努力」這件事拿掉。傳給對睡眠這件事很有壓力的朋友。
variations: 今晚不用拚命睡，把燈關掉，把自己交給床就好。

en.content:
The night doesn't owe you a good sleep,
and you don't owe the night a performance of trying hard to sleep.
Lights off, lie down — that's already enough.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs spiral into 'I must try harder to sleep,' which only keeps them awake. This removes the effort entirely. Send to the friend who treats bedtime as a test.
variations: Don't fight for sleep tonight. Turn the light off and let the bed do the rest.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'at 3am I'm an unsupervised genius full of ideas I'll forget by morning' meme; rewritten with a localized 'no boss, no notepad' twist

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點的我，是一個沒人管的天才。
想出三個改變人生的點子、
一首要爆紅的歌詞、
還有一個事業計畫。
早上起來，一個都記不得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「半夜靈感無敵、醒來腦袋空白」的全民通病的精準描繪。傳給創作者朋友，他們會回「我也常常損失幾百萬」。
variations: 深夜的我是天才，白天的我是他的失憶秘書。

en.content:
At 3am, I'm an unsupervised genius.
Three life-changing ideas,
a song lyric that's about to go viral,
and a full business plan.
By morning, I remember exactly none of them.
en.editorNote: Pinpoint description of the universal 'midnight genius, morning amnesia' bug. Send to a creator friend — they'll reply 'I lose millions this way every week.'
variations: Night me is a genius. Morning me is his forgetful assistant.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'mapping ceiling cracks like constellations' meme angle; rewritten as amateur astronomer framing

zh-tw.content:
躺到第四個小時，我已經把天花板上每一道裂痕當成星座命名完畢。
左上角是「水漬大熊座」，
右邊那條是「上次粉刷沒刷好座」。
睡不著的副業，是業餘天文學家。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把長時間瞪天花板包裝成「天文觀測」的反諷金句。畫面感很強，適合配張天花板細節照當限動。
variations: 我家天花板有自己的星圖，是我四點獨家命名的。

en.content:
Four hours in, I've named every crack on the ceiling like a constellation.
Top-left is the Great Water-Stain Bear,
the long one on the right is the Botched-Paint-Job Belt.
My side hustle, while not sleeping: amateur astronomer.
en.editorNote: Reframes the ceiling-stare marathon as serious astronomy. Highly visual — pair with a tight ceiling-detail photo for the perfect 4am story.
variations: My ceiling has its own star chart. I drew it at 4am, exclusively.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the viral '我最近失眠，16小時才醒一次' joke format; rewritten with an HR-style accounting twist

zh-tw.content:
別人問我最近睡眠怎樣，
我說：還好，我每 16 小時才醒一次。
他們以為我在炫耀，
其實我每天只睡 4 小時。
zh-tw.editorNote: 經典反諷數學梗的進階版，講完才會被反應過來「等等不對」。適合在朋友抱怨睡不好時神回。
variations: 我的睡眠統計：睡得少、醒得多、總量還在虧損。

en.content:
When people ask how my sleep's been lately,
I tell them: not bad, I only wake up every 16 hours.
They think I'm bragging,
but I only sleep four hours a day.
en.editorNote: An upgraded version of the classic ironic-math joke — it takes a beat to land. Perfect deadpan reply when a friend complains about their sleep.
variations: Sleep audit: low input, high wake count, still operating at a loss.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'sleep deprivation proudly sponsored by my thoughts' tweet; rewritten as a full TV-style sponsor read

zh-tw.content:
本日的失眠，由「你六年前那句話」獨家贊助。
感謝協力廠商「明天的會議」、「沒回的訊息」、「冰箱裡那塊蛋糕」。
節目進行中，歡迎繼續收看。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把腦內反芻包裝成電視冠名贊助的反諷金句。社畜、追劇族都會懂，傳到群組會有人補「下一檔贊助商：去年那次離職」。
variations: 今晚的失眠由我自己的思緒全程贊助播出，請鎖定 03:47 高潮回放。

en.content:
Tonight's insomnia is proudly sponsored by "that thing you said six years ago."
With additional support from "tomorrow's meeting," "the text you didn't reply to," and "that cake in the fridge."
We now return to our regularly scheduled overthinking.
en.editorNote: Reframes 3am rumination as a televised sponsor read. Office workers and binge-watchers both get it instantly — expect replies adding their own 'sponsors.'
variations: Tonight's insomnia is brought to you, in full, by my own thoughts. Stay tuned for the 3:47am replay.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'breath instead of sleep' angle to the healing register

zh-tw.content:
如果今晚睡不著，那就先好好呼吸。
吸氣、吐氣，吸氣、吐氣。
你已經在做一件很重要的事了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠時最容易忘記「光是活著、好好呼吸已經算數」。這句話把標準拉到最低，讓人能在床上鬆一口氣。傳給最近壓力很大睡不好的朋友。
variations: 睡不著沒關係，記得好好呼吸，這就夠了。

en.content:
If you can't sleep tonight, just breathe for now.
In, out, in, out.
You're already doing something important.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs forget that simply breathing counts. This sets the bar at the floor so you can actually exhale in bed. For a friend running on stress and bad sleep.
variations: Sleep can wait. Just breathe — that's already enough.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'insomnia gives you time to win every argument in your head' tweet; rewritten with a courtroom rematch framing

zh-tw.content:
失眠最大的好處，是讓我重新打贏這輩子每一場吵架。
凌晨三點的我，口才絕佳、邏輯通順、句句致命。
可惜對方都不在場。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「半夜才想到當時應該怎麼回」的全民通病的精準補刀。傳給總是事後才想到神回覆的朋友，他們會回「我剛剛也贏了一場」。
variations: 我深夜的辯論能力，是白天的我永遠到不了的境界。

en.content:
The best thing about insomnia is that I get to win every argument I've ever had, again.
3am me has razor-sharp comebacks, perfect logic, and the killing blow.
Unfortunately, the other party isn't in the room.
en.editorNote: Surgical takedown of the universal 'thought of the perfect reply six hours too late' bug. Send to the friend who always finishes arguments in the shower — expect 'I just won one too.'
variations: My late-night debate skills are a level daytime me will never reach.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'furious with each pillow individually' viral tweet; rewritten with a localized hostage-negotiation framing

zh-tw.content:
失眠到第三個小時，我已經跟我的枕頭一個一個談判過。
太軟、太硬、太熱、太涼。
沒有一個願意配合，我們進入冷戰階段。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「翻來覆去」描繪成跟枕頭的多方談判，畫面感拉滿。適合在又被枕頭背叛的清晨發限動。
variations: 我家的枕頭組成了反睡眠聯盟，每晚輪流值班。

en.content:
Three hours into insomnia, I've negotiated with each of my pillows one by one.
Too soft, too hard, too hot, too cold.
None of them will cooperate. We're now in a cold war.
en.editorNote: Turns the toss-and-turn marathon into a multi-party pillow negotiation. Highly visual — perfect caption for the morning after another pillow betrayal.
variations: My pillows have formed an anti-sleep coalition and they rotate shifts.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by '什麼都在退步，只有熬夜在進步' viral line; rewritten with a localized year-end review framing

zh-tw.content:
今年的年度回顧：
體力退步、記性退步、視力退步。
唯一進步的，是熬夜的續航力。
要不要去申請金氏世界紀錄？
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「全身都在崩」這件事講得很有成就感的反諷金句。年末發特別應景，傳到朋友群組會引發一片「我也是」。
variations: 我這幾年退步的項目排成一列，只有熬夜這項升等。

en.content:
My year in review:
Stamina down, memory down, eyesight down.
The only thing trending up is my ability to stay awake past 3am.
Should I apply for a world record?
en.editorNote: Reframes total bodily decline as a single hard-earned achievement. Lethal end-of-year material — drop in the group chat for a chorus of 'same.'
variations: Everything I have is on a downward graph except staying up late.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'sleep is cheaper than therapy' format; rewritten with a localized invoice-style breakdown

zh-tw.content:
本月支出：
咖啡 NT$2,400、能量飲 NT$800、眼霜 NT$1,500、按摩 NT$1,200。
如果當初好好睡覺，這些錢都不用花。
我的失眠是一個小型經濟體。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把熬夜的代價算成發票明細，特別有畫面。社畜、月光族都會苦笑，傳同事群組會有人補「我這個月還要加眼藥水」。
variations: 我每個月為了補救睡不著，多花的錢可以再買一張床。

en.content:
This month's expenses:
Coffee $80, energy drinks $25, eye cream $50, massage $40.
If I had just slept, none of this would exist.
My insomnia is a small economy of its own.
en.editorNote: Repackages the cost of bad sleep as an itemized invoice. Painfully relatable for office workers — expect coworkers to add 'plus eye drops this month.'
variations: What I spend each month patching up bad sleep could buy me a new bed.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'brain wakes me at 3am to review my worst decisions' format; rewritten with a localized 'unscheduled board meeting' framing

zh-tw.content:
我的腦袋每天凌晨三點，
都會自動召開一場「人生重大決策檢討會」。
與會者只有我一個，主席也是我，
而且每次結論都是：你完蛋了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 凌晨三點突然清醒、開始檢討人生的精準描述。傳到朋友群組會引發一連串「同款主席」的回覆，社畜共鳴度爆表。
variations: 我沒有失眠，我只是被自己排了一場凌晨三點的內部會議。

en.content:
Every night at 3am, my brain calls an emergency board meeting
to review all my major life decisions.
There's only one attendee, I'm also the chair,
and the conclusion is always: you're cooked.
en.editorNote: A pinpoint take on the unprompted 3am self-review. Drop it in the group chat and watch everyone confirm they chair the same meeting nightly.
variations: I don't have insomnia. My brain just scheduled a 3am all-hands without telling me.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'sleep no longer cures tiredness, it just postpones it' viral meme; rewritten with a localized loan-and-interest metaphor

zh-tw.content:
以前睡覺是充電，
現在睡覺只是把累延後幾個小時。
感覺我不是在休息，
是在跟身體借錢，而且還有利息。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「睡了還是累」的崩潰感講成欠債的反諷金句。社畜、爸媽、加班族都會苦笑點頭，傳同事群組會引發一片「我這筆已經滾很久了」。
variations: 睡覺只是把累的帳單往後推，利息照算。

en.content:
Sleep used to recharge me.
Now it just postpones the exhaustion by a few hours.
It's not rest anymore —
it's borrowing energy from my body, with interest.
en.editorNote: Reframes 'still tired after sleeping' as ballooning debt. Lethal for office workers and tired parents — expect a chorus of 'my interest has been compounding for years.'
variations: Sleep just pushes the exhaustion bill down the road. The interest keeps adding up.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'brain finally gets to speak without interruption' viral tweet format; rewritten with a 'mic finally on' framing

zh-tw.content:
白天我的腦袋一直被開會、訊息、紅綠燈打斷。
到了凌晨兩點，它終於拿到麥克風，
然後第一句就講了我十年前的丟臉事。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「白天忙到沒空想、晚上腦袋大爆發」精準描繪出來。適合凌晨被回憶突襲時發限動，配張黑黑的房間照特別有氣氛。
variations: 我的腦袋白天都在排隊，凌晨兩點才終於輪到它講話。

en.content:
All day my brain gets interrupted by meetings, messages, and traffic lights.
At 2am it finally gets handed the mic,
and the first thing it brings up is something embarrassing from ten years ago.
en.editorNote: Captures the 'too busy by day, full blast by night' insomnia perfectly. Great late-night caption — pair with a dark-room photo for maximum mood.
variations: My brain spends all day in queue. 2am is when it finally gets called up to speak.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'how to sleep 8 hours in 4 hours' meme; rewritten with a localized speed-run framing

zh-tw.content:
別人睡八小時叫睡眠，
我睡四小時叫速通。
隔天上班的我，就是那個遊戲卡關卡到當機的角色。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡眠不足講成電玩速通的反諷金句。年輕族群、玩家圈、加班族都會笑著轉發，傳朋友群組會引發一片「我也卡 BUG 中」。
variations: 別人睡眠正常通關，我屬於用四小時硬刷的速通組。

en.content:
Other people sleep eight hours. That's called sleeping.
I sleep four hours. That's called a speedrun.
Next-day-me is the character who freezes mid-quest and crashes.
en.editorNote: Repackages sleep deprivation as a videogame speedrun. Easy hit with gamers and overworked employees — expect replies of 'I'm currently soft-locked.'
variations: Normal people complete sleep. I'm doing it any% with all glitches enabled.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'tonight doesn't have to be productive' angle to the healing register

zh-tw.content:
睡不著的這個夜晚，
你不用變更好，也不用想通什麼。
你只是在這裡，
這樣就已經夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人最常邊躺邊覺得「我連睡覺都做不好」。這句話只是把那種自我責備關掉。傳給最近躺著就會內耗的朋友。
variations: 睡不著沒關係，不用順便要求自己想通人生。

en.content:
On a night you can't sleep,
you don't have to become better or figure anything out.
You're just here.
That's already enough.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs often spiral into 'I can't even sleep right.' This quietly turns that self-blame off. For the friend whose bed has become a courtroom.
variations: Not sleeping is fine. You don't also have to use the time to solve your life.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'I'm not tired, I'm in a 72-hour disagreement with my bed' line; rewritten with a localized lease-dispute framing

zh-tw.content:
我不是不想睡，
是我跟床正在進行一場為期七天的合約糾紛。
它說它沒義務讓我睡，
我說我也沒義務每天去躺。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把失眠包裝成「跟床的法律攻防戰」，反差感製造笑點。適合在連續好幾天睡不好時發限動，朋友會搶著留言「我也想解約」。
variations: 我跟床的關係，目前進入冷戰第七天。

en.content:
I'm not refusing to sleep.
My bed and I are just deep into a week-long contract dispute.
It claims it has no obligation to let me sleep.
I claim I have no obligation to keep showing up.
en.editorNote: Frames insomnia as a legal standoff with your own bed. The absurd reframe is the punchline. Post after a string of bad nights and watch friends pile in with 'mine breached too.'
variations: Me and my bed are on day seven of the silent treatment.

items.sourceNote: Original item — reframes the overactive nighttime brain as a 24-hour convenience store that never closes

zh-tw.content:
白天的腦袋是公務機關，下午五點準時打烊。
半夜的腦袋是 24 小時超商，
而且還主打「黑歷史限定回憶口味」，全年無休。
zh-tw.editorNote: 用「超商 vs 公家機關」對比白天黑夜腦袋運作的差別，畫面感很台灣。深夜被腦袋拉去翻舊帳時最適合發。
variations: 我的腦袋是夜班最認真的員工，白天一直打瞌睡。

en.content:
Daytime brain is a government office — locks up at 5pm sharp.
Midnight brain is a 24-hour convenience store,
and tonight's special is 'limited-edition cringe memory flavor,' available all year.
en.editorNote: Sharp contrast between the lazy day brain and the wildly productive night brain. Post it when your mind has once again opened the regret aisle at 2am.
variations: My brain works its hardest shift overnight and naps through the day.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'a warm glass of regret cures insomnia' line; rewritten with a localized late-night drink-menu metaphor

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點，腦袋遞上今晚的菜單：
一杯溫的後悔、一份冰的尷尬，
還有一碗熱騰騰、剛剛才煮好的擔心。
我說：謝謝，全部來一份。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「凌晨三點的負面思緒」包裝成深夜菜單，畫面感很強。傳給最近常被腦內回憶夜襲的朋友，會引發一片「我也點全套」。
variations: 深夜限定套餐：後悔加焦慮，附贈一份十年前的丟臉。

en.content:
3am, my brain hands me tonight's menu:
a warm cup of regret, a cold side of awkwardness,
and a steaming bowl of fresh worry just off the stove.
I say: thanks, I'll take one of each.
en.editorNote: Repackages 3am intrusive thoughts as a late-night tasting menu. Perfect caption after a night your brain served the full course — friends will reply 'same order.'
variations: Tonight's special: regret with a side of awkward, and embarrassment for dessert.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'tonight you don't have to win, just keep yourself company' angle to the healing register

zh-tw.content:
今晚不用打贏睡眠這場仗。
你只要陪自己一下，
摸摸自己的頭，跟自己說一聲：
辛苦了，今天能撐到這裡，已經很厲害。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人最常把睡覺當成「必須贏的考試」，越想越睡不著。這句話把那個競爭關掉。傳給最近一躺下就壓力爆表的朋友。
variations: 今晚不用睡得多好，只要好好陪自己一下就行了。

en.content:
You don't have to win the sleep battle tonight.
Just keep yourself company for a little while.
Pat your own head and tell yourself:
you made it this far today — that's already a lot.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs often turn sleep into a test they have to ace, which only makes it worse. This switches that competition off. For the friend whose bedtime has become a pressure cooker.
variations: Tonight doesn't have to be a good sleep. It just has to be a kind one.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'tired but wired, make it make sense' viral line; rewritten with a localized 'two opposing operating systems' framing

zh-tw.content:
我身上同時運行兩套作業系統：
眼皮的版本叫「累爆」，
腦袋的版本叫「超清醒」。
兩套系統互不相容，目前正在當機。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「明明累成狗、腦袋卻超亮」這種失眠雙重狀態的精準描述。傳到朋友群組會引發一片「我這台也跑兩個系統」。
variations: 我的身體跟腦袋分別住在不同時區。

en.content:
I'm currently running two operating systems at once:
the eyelid version is called "completely wrecked,"
the brain version is called "weirdly alert."
The two are incompatible. We are now crashing.
en.editorNote: Pinpoint description of the 'tired but wired' insomnia paradox. Drop in the group chat — someone will reply 'mine dual-boots every night.'
variations: My body and my brain live in completely different time zones.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my bed: 10/10, my brain: 0/10 timing' viral line; rewritten with a localized restaurant review format

zh-tw.content:
床的評價：五顆星，柔軟、剛剛好、無可挑剔。
腦袋的時機：一顆星，永遠選在最不該想的時候開機。
綜合體驗：建議下次直接放棄。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把睡覺體驗包裝成 Google 評論的反諷金句。畫面感很台灣，傳到朋友群組會有人補「我家床也滿分但腦袋是奧客」。
variations: 床是滿分商品，腦袋是退貨理由。

en.content:
Bed review: five stars, soft, just right, flawless.
Brain timing: one star, always boots up at the worst possible moment.
Overall experience: maybe just skip the visit next time.
en.editorNote: Reframes a bad night's sleep as a Yelp review of yourself. Friends will reply 'my bed got five stars too but my brain is the difficult customer.'
variations: The bed is the best product I own. The brain is the reason for the return.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'my brain has officially gone on strike' tweet format; rewritten with a localized union dispute framing

zh-tw.content:
凌晨四點，我的腦袋宣布罷工。
訴求：要求加薪、減少加班、停止深夜開會。
資方代表：也是我。
談判破裂，預計明天繼續抗爭。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把腦袋停不下來的反芻包裝成勞資爭議的反諷金句。社畜共鳴爆棚，傳同事群組會有人接「我們同集團」。
variations: 我的腦袋發動了一場沒有結果的勞資協商。

en.content:
At 4am, my brain officially went on strike.
Demands: a raise, less overtime, no more midnight meetings.
Management representative: also me.
Negotiations broke down. Picketing will continue tomorrow.
en.editorNote: Reframes the unstoppable late-night brain as a labor dispute. Office workers will instantly relate — expect 'we're in the same union' replies.
variations: My brain started a labor negotiation that goes nowhere every single night.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'small kindnesses for the awake version of you' angle to the healing register

zh-tw.content:
如果今晚還醒著，
幫醒著的那個自己做點小事：
喝一口溫水、把棉被拉好、燈調暗一點。
你不用睡著，也可以對自己溫柔。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人常常覺得「睡不著的我不值得照顧」，硬撐到天亮。這句話把「照顧自己」的門檻拉到很低。傳給最近躺在床上會自我譴責的朋友。
variations: 睡不著的夜晚，也可以對醒著的自己好一點。

en.content:
If you're still awake tonight,
do something small for the awake version of you:
sip warm water, tug the blanket up, dim the light a notch.
You don't need to fall asleep to be kind to yourself.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs often feel the 'awake me' doesn't deserve care and just power through. This drops the bar for self-kindness to the floor. For a friend who lies in bed beating themselves up.
variations: On a sleepless night, you're still allowed to be gentle with the version of you that's awake.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'tired all day, wide awake the second I lie down' viral meme angle; rewritten with a localized motion-sensor framing

zh-tw.content:
白天累到走路像漫遊，眼睛要黏起來。
一躺到床上，身體立刻偵測到平面，
自動切換成「全頻道清醒模式」。
我的床比我還警覺。
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「累到不行躺下就清醒」這個全民通病的精準描述。傳給也常常一沾枕頭就突然像吃了能量飲的朋友。
variations: 我躺下的那一秒，腦袋自動開機，比 Wi-Fi 還快。

en.content:
All day I shuffle around like I'm in low-power mode, eyes glued shut.
The second I lie down, my body detects a flat surface
and instantly switches to 'all channels alert mode.'
My bed is more vigilant than I am.
en.editorNote: Pinpoint take on the universal 'exhausted all day, instantly awake in bed' bug. Send to the friend who also boots up the moment their head hits the pillow.
variations: The second I lie down, my brain powers on faster than my Wi-Fi.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'day three of no sleep, I'm having philosophical breakthroughs' meme; rewritten with a localized TED talk framing

zh-tw.content:
連續睡不好的第三天，
我在電梯裡突然頓悟了宇宙的真理。
本來想去演 TED Talk，
結果走出電梯就忘了，只記得自己很想吃蛋餅。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把缺覺三天的「假性開悟」狀態講得超寫實。社畜、熬夜爸媽、考生都會苦笑。傳同事群組會引發「我昨天也悟道」一片。
variations: 缺覺第三天，我以為自己變聰明了，其實只是腦袋當機。

en.content:
Day three of bad sleep,
I had a breakthrough about the meaning of the universe in the elevator.
I was going to do a TED Talk about it,
but by the time the doors opened I'd forgotten everything except that I wanted a breakfast sandwich.
en.editorNote: Nails the fake-enlightenment phase of sleep deprivation. Lethal for office workers, exhausted parents, and students alike — expect a 'I had a breakthrough yesterday too' reply.
variations: On day three of no sleep, I thought I'd gotten smarter. My brain had just crashed.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by 'weekend sleep debt collector shows up Monday morning' meme angle; rewritten with a localized aggressive-creditor framing

zh-tw.content:
週六睡到中午，我以為帳已經還清。
週日加碼睡到下午三點，我以為有結餘。
週一早上鬧鐘響，討債公司直接登門：
「妳這禮拜還欠十二個小時。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「補眠根本補不回來」的崩潰感講成被討債的反諷金句。社畜共鳴度爆表，傳同事群組會有人接「我這筆已經滾兩年了」。
variations: 我以為週末補眠是還款，沒想到是預支下週的疲憊。

en.content:
Saturday I slept till noon — I thought the debt was cleared.
Sunday I slept till 3pm — I thought I had savings.
Monday morning the alarm rings, and the sleep debt collector is at the door:
"You still owe twelve hours this week."
en.editorNote: Repackages the impossible math of weekend catch-up sleep as an aggressive debt collection scene. Lethal for office workers — drop in the team chat for 'mine's been compounding for two years' replies.
variations: I thought weekend sleep was a payment. Turns out it was just borrowing from next week.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'tonight's only job is to lie down' angle to the healing register

zh-tw.content:
今晚唯一的待辦事項，是躺好。
不用排明天的行程，不用回想今天的對話，
不用解決那個還沒想通的問題。
你今晚的工作，已經完成了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人最容易在床上順便處理整個人生。這句話把「今晚」的任務縮到只剩躺下。傳給最近在床上還在開會、回訊息的工作狂朋友。
variations: 今晚只要躺著就好，其他的事都不歸你管。

en.content:
Tonight's only to-do is to lie down.
Not plan tomorrow's schedule, not replay today's conversations,
not solve the problem you haven't figured out yet.
Your job for tonight is already done.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs love to use bedtime to process their entire life. This shrinks tonight's task to a single line. For the workaholic friend still holding meetings in their head after midnight.
variations: Tonight, your only job is to lie there. Everything else isn't on your plate.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'tired all day, suddenly wired at bedtime' viral tweet phenomenon; rewritten with a localized power-supply framing

zh-tw.content:
整個白天：我快沒電了，誰來幫我充一下。
晚上十一點上床：欸不對，我現在電力 100%，要不要乾脆規劃一下明年的人生？
zh-tw.editorNote: 對「累到不行卻一躺下就滿血」這種失眠雙重人格的精準描繪。傳給也常常半夜突然超有精神的朋友，他們會回「這是我每天的劇本」。
variations: 我的身體是那種白天紅電、半夜滿格的爛電池。
白天的我求充電，半夜的我想開公司。

en.content:
All day: I'm about to die, somebody plug me in.
11pm in bed: wait, actually I'm at 100% now — should I just go ahead and plan out next year?
en.editorNote: A pinpoint take on the insomnia split personality where you're dead all day and fully charged the second you lie down. Send to the friend who also gets a second wind at midnight — they'll reply 'this is my nightly script.'
variations: My body is the kind of cheap battery that's red all day and full bars at midnight.
Daytime me begs to be charged. Midnight me wants to launch a startup.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the '3am brain googles how do penguins hug and now I'm crying' meme; rewritten with a localized late-night rabbit-hole framing

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點，我本來只是想查一個小問題。
半小時後，我已經哭著看完一支「老狗等主人回家」的影片，
還順便查了企鵝到底怎麼抱抱。
問題解決了嗎？沒有。我更清醒了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「深夜查一個問題結果掉進無底洞」的全民通病講得超寫實。傳給也常常半夜被演算法帶走的朋友，他們會回「我昨天也哭了」。
variations: 我只是想查一件事，結果天亮了，我變成企鵝專家。
凌晨的搜尋紀錄，是另一個版本的我留下的犯罪現場。

en.content:
3am, I just wanted to look up one small thing.
Half an hour later I'm crying through a video of an old dog waiting for its owner,
and I've also looked up how penguins actually hug.
Did I solve the original problem? No. I'm just more awake now.
en.editorNote: Nails the universal 'looked up one thing and fell into the abyss' bug. Send to the friend who also gets hauled off by the algorithm at night — they'll reply 'I cried last night too.'
variations: I just wanted to check one thing. The sun came up and now I'm a penguin expert.
My late-night search history is a crime scene left by a different version of me.

items.sourceNote: Inspired by the 'me: we need to sleep / brain: let's review every decision' viral format; rewritten with a localized 'brain refuses to clock out' framing

zh-tw.content:
我：好了，今天到此為止，我們睡覺。
腦袋：等等，不如我們來重看一次今天你講的每一句話？
我：拜託，你下班了。
腦袋：我是責任制，沒有下班這種東西。
zh-tw.editorNote: 把「想睡腦袋卻不肯停」的拉扯包裝成勞資對話，社畜共鳴爆棚。傳同事群組會有人接「我這台也是責任制」。
variations: 我跟腦袋約好十一點關機，它每次都自己偷偷加班到三點。
我的腦袋是公司裡唯一不準下班的員工，老闆還是它自己。

en.content:
Me: Okay, that's it for today, we're going to sleep.
Brain: Hold on, how about we rewatch every single thing you said today?
Me: Please, you're off the clock.
Brain: I'm salaried. There's no such thing as off the clock.
en.editorNote: Frames the 'I want to sleep but my brain won't stop' tug-of-war as a labor dispute. Lethal for office workers — drop in the team chat for 'mine's salaried too' replies.
variations: Brain and I agreed on a 11pm shutdown. It secretly works overtime until 3am every night.
My brain is the one employee that's never allowed to clock out, and it's also the boss.

items.sourceNote: Original gentle item — adds a 'you're allowed to put the night down' angle to the healing register

zh-tw.content:
你今晚扛的那些事，可以先放在床邊。
它們不會跑掉，明天還在。
但現在這一刻，你可以先什麼都不扛，
只是躺著，當一個被夜晚接住的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 失眠的人常常把整個明天都搬上床一起扛。這句話允許你先把重量放下。傳給最近一躺下就開始想全部煩惱的朋友。
variations: 今晚你可以先把所有煩惱放在門口，明天再領回來。
你不用整夜抱著明天，現在先讓自己被床接住就好。

en.content:
The things you're carrying tonight can be set down beside the bed.
They won't run off — they'll still be here tomorrow.
But for this moment, you don't have to hold anything.
Just lie there, and let the night catch you.
en.editorNote: Insomniacs tend to drag all of tomorrow into bed and carry it at once. This gives permission to put the weight down for now. For the friend who starts holding every worry the moment they lie down.
variations: Tonight you can leave all your worries at the door and pick them back up tomorrow.
You don't have to hold tomorrow all night. For now, just let the bed catch you.
